topic: exact no-cancellation compatibility frontier for simultaneous NUC and study-exchangeability sensitivity: characterize K(P) by C_P(rho,gamma)=max_t E[(L_t-U_t)_+], and derive cross-fitted nuisance-robust contact-set inference uniformly over the two-parameter frontier including interval ties, with the verified two-stratum cancellation witness and the regular local separation boundary; fixes the average-overlap/fixed-nuisance incompatibility test used by Lanners et al. (NeurIPS 2025) in Project STAR

open_problems.open_problem: What is the exact population compatibility set for simultaneous NUC and study-exchangeability sensitivity, and is it characterized without cancellation by K(P) = {(rho,gamma): max_t E[(L_t(X;rho,gamma)-U_t(X;rho,gamma))_+]=0}?

source_refs.claim: The paper defines compatibility through conditional interval overlap but replaces pointwise checking by an expectation-level diagnostic because direct pointwise verification is infeasible.
source_refs.author: Quinn Lanners, Cynthia Rudin, Alexander Volfovsky, Harsh Parikh

source_refs.claim: Its no-free-lunch result says consistent fusion requires correctly distinguishing failures of external validity from failures of ignorability, but it does not characterize simultaneous failures.
source_refs.author: Harsh Parikh, Marco Morucci, Vittorio Orlandi, Sudeepa Roy, Cynthia Rudin, Alexander Volfovsky

source_refs.excerpt: The identification rung is conditional on joint feasibility

open_problems.why_unsolved: Lanners et al. formulate compatibility as conditional interval overlap but operationalize it with an averaged signed discrepancy, which can cancel across covariate strata even when conditional intervals are disjoint on sets of positive probability. The banked joint-misspecification proposal independently assumes that two latent-law restrictions have a nonempty intersection and derives endpoints only conditional on that feasibility. Neither source supplies a necessary-and-sufficient observed-law characterization preserving the conditional constraints, so separate or average feasibility can paste together incompatible latent laws.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient zero-set characterization of K(P) by a nonnegative conditional-violation functional, plus a two-stratum counterexample proving that the legacy signed-average criterion can vanish under incompatibility.

writing_exemplar.why: Its narrative already moves from complementary RCT and observational weaknesses to joint sensitivity parameters and an empirical compatibility region, allowing a direct pivot at the averaged diagnostic.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: RCTs provide internal validity while observational cohorts provide representativeness -> simultaneous NUC and study-exchangeability failures require two sensitivity parameters -> admissible parameter pairs must be compatible with the observed conditional laws -> characterize compatibility exactly without cross-stratum cancellation

method_exemplars.technique: Represent conditional feasibility as covariate-indexed linear programs and use primal-dual characterizations before aggregating across covariates.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Exact compatibility is a conditional feasibility problem; the conditional-LP argument exposes why averaging constraints before checking feasibility loses sharpness.

method_exemplars.technique: Convert a continuum of inequalities into an intersection-bound functional and use Gaussian-process approximation with adaptive inequality selection.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The zero set of the maximum overlap violation is an intersection/contact-set object indexed by sensitivity parameters.

open_problems.open_problem: How can one construct cross-fitted, nuisance-robust confidence regions for the exact two-parameter compatibility set and its contact frontier that remain uniformly valid when treatment arms, interval endpoints, or active faces tie?

source_refs.claim: The paper states that its compatibility resampling holds fitted propensity and outcome functions fixed, understates variability, and may enlarge the reported incompatible region; nuisance uncertainty is left for future work.
source_refs.author: Quinn Lanners, Cynthia Rudin, Alexander Volfovsky, Harsh Parikh

source_refs.claim: The paper derives uniform confidence bands for a multidimensional breakdown frontier, but for explicitly characterized smooth bound functions rather than a cross-fitted max-positive-part compatibility functional.
source_refs.author: Matthew A. Masten, Alexandre Poirier

source_refs.claim: The paper shows that max-type causal value functionals become nonregular at exceptional laws where competing treatment choices tie on a positive-probability stratum.
source_refs.author: Alexander R. Luedtke, Mark J. van der Laan

source_refs.excerpt: directional-differentiability fallback as a separate open-ended question with active-set switching
add an explicit external sample/model and the corresponding influence contribution for Q0hat_a.

open_problems.why_unsolved: The target combines learned propensity, outcome, and possibly external-study envelope nuisances with a nonsmooth positive-part/max map and a two-dimensional zero set. Cross-fitting alone controls smooth orthogonal-score remainders, not the directional derivative when treatment arms, interval endpoints, or contact faces tie; ordinary bootstrap bands may fail for directionally but not fully differentiable maps. The prior reviewer prescriptions independently identify both missing pieces: an external-sample influence contribution and a directional-differentiability fallback for active-set switching.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A cross-fitted orthogonal estimator of the compatibility process and a contact-set or numerical-delta multiplier bootstrap with a uniform coverage theorem over the sensitivity rectangle, including positive-mass ties and an explicit external-sample influence term when the study envelope is estimated.

writing_exemplar.why: Its compatibility plots and explicit fixed-nuisance caveat provide the closest route from an applied robustness region to the missing coverage guarantee.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Two sensitivity parameters produce an empirically useful compatibility map -> the map depends on learned propensity, outcome, and study nuisances -> fixed-nuisance resampling can overstate incompatibility -> obtain uniform nuisance-robust contact-set inference even when active constraints tie

method_exemplars.technique: Construct Neyman-orthogonal scores, cross-fit nuisance estimates, prove product-rate remainder bounds, and bootstrap the asymptotically linear process.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the missing nuisance-uncertainty propagation, while the compatibility application requires extending its smooth-target theory to an indexed nonsmooth process.

method_exemplars.technique: Compute the Hadamard directional derivative of a nonsmooth map and apply a derivative-aware bootstrap instead of the inconsistent ordinary bootstrap.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Positive parts, maxima, and zero-level contact sets are directionally differentiable maps whose derivative changes at interval or arm ties.

method_exemplars.technique: Use Gaussian approximation and adaptive contact selection to form uniform bands for extrema indexed by a continuum.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Uniform inference over a two-parameter rectangle requires controlling an indexed max process and estimating which constraints are near binding.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the uniform local separation boundary for testing exact compatibility as C_{P_n}(rho,gamma) approaches zero, and how do positive-mass interval ties or cancellation in the legacy signed statistic change the attainable detection rate and local power?

source_refs.claim: The paper notes that fixed-nuisance resampling produces an overly narrow null distribution but gives no separation-rate or local-power analysis near the compatibility frontier.
source_refs.author: Quinn Lanners, Cynthia Rudin, Alexander Volfovsky, Harsh Parikh

source_refs.claim: The paper shows that moment-inequality procedures need uniform validity across changing sets of binding inequalities and derives local-power gains from generalized moment selection.
source_refs.author: Donald W. K. Andrews, Gustavo Soares

source_refs.claim: The paper identifies nonunique optima on positive-probability strata as exceptional laws where standard pathwise differentiability and regular inference can fail.
source_refs.author: Alexander R. Luedtke, Mark J. van der Laan

open_problems.why_unsolved: Under regular separation, a unique active arm and a margin away from interval ties may produce root-n local behavior. If the conditional gap is zero on positive mass or the active set changes, the tangent experiment and detection rate can change; cancellation additionally makes the legacy signed statistic locally blind to some alternatives. Existing moment-inequality and frontier results do not combine this geometry with cross-fitted causal nuisances, and the anchor supplies neither a minimax lower bound nor an attaining test.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A regime-indexed minimax separation lower bound and an attaining compatibility test, with a local-power limit under regular n^{-1/2} separation and an explicit slower-rate or nonregular characterization at interval-tie laws.

writing_exemplar.why: Its incompatible-region classification makes errors near the plotted frontier substantively visible and motivates asking which local violations can be detected.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Sensitivity plots classify pairs as compatible or incompatible -> conclusions depend on pairs near the boundary -> fixed-nuisance resampling distorts boundary uncertainty -> determine detectable local incompatibility with and without ties

method_exemplars.technique: Select near-binding inequalities with a drifting threshold, prove uniform null size, and calculate power against generalized local alternatives.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Treatment arms and covariate strata that nearly violate overlap play the role of drifting binding moments.

method_exemplars.technique: Separate regular from exceptional laws by pathwise differentiability and stabilize one-step estimation when the optimizer is nonunique.
method_exemplars.why_closest: An interval tie is structurally analogous to a nonunique optimal action because the derivative of the max/positive-part map depends on a nonunique active set.

method_exemplars.technique: Invert uniform bands for a multidimensional breakdown frontier and analyze its directionally differentiable bound maps.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired power calculation lives on a two-parameter robustness frontier, here specialized to a compatibility zero set with tie nonregularity.

open_problems.open_problem: For bounded outcomes, what are the support-aware sharp ATE and CATE bounds under simultaneous (rho,gamma) violations, and what finite-alpha envelope relates the Boltzmann-smoothed endpoints to those sharp bounds?

source_refs.claim: The paper explicitly leaves sharper bounds using known outcome support and a formal finite-alpha characterization of its Boltzmann approximation error for future work.
source_refs.author: Quinn Lanners, Cynthia Rudin, Alexander Volfovsky, Harsh Parikh

source_refs.claim: The paper derives sharp covariate-assisted IV bounds and handles nonsmooth extrema with margin conditions or smooth approximations, while leaving optimal tuning and minimax behavior of smoothing open.
source_refs.author: Alexander W. Levis, Matteo Bonvini, Zhenghao Zeng, Luke Keele, Edward H. Kennedy
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B

source_refs.claim: The paper develops debiased exact and entropically regularized estimators for conditional linear-program bounds, exposing the exact-versus-smoothed tradeoff in an adjacent partial-identification problem.
source_refs.author: Eli Ben-Michael

open_problems.why_unsolved: The anchor's ratio restrictions generate conditional extrema but do not fully intersect them with known outcome support, so the reported interval need not be support-aware sharp. Its estimator targets a Boltzmann-smoothed surrogate; convergence as alpha diverges does not quantify finite-alpha identification error or the tradeoff between smoothing bias and sampling error. Adjacent conditional-LP and IV-bound work does not solve the simultaneous NUC and study-exchangeability constraint system.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A support-constrained sharp identified interval for ATE and CATE, a deterministic finite-alpha bound on the smoothing gap, and an inference-valid tuning rule balancing smoothing bias with stochastic error.

writing_exemplar.why: It defines the two sensitivity parameters and explicitly identifies both missing support-aware sharpness and missing finite-smoothing theory.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: Simultaneous assumption failures yield sensitivity bounds -> bounded outcomes add logically valid restrictions -> current extrema and Boltzmann surrogates do not certify finite-alpha support-aware sharpness -> derive the sharp interval and approximation envelope

method_exemplars.technique: Encode support and sensitivity restrictions as conditional linear programs, recover exact values through primal-dual solutions, and compare them with entropic regularization.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The sharp bound is an expectation of covariate-indexed constrained optima, and the finite-alpha question mirrors exact versus regularized conditional LPs.

method_exemplars.technique: Use a margin condition to control active-set mistakes, derive influence-function estimators for exact bounds, and alternatively estimate log-sum-exp approximations under explicit nuisance-rate conditions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Both targets are expectations of pointwise min/max formulas with learned nuisances, so its exact-versus-smoothed inference split transfers directly.

method_exemplars.technique: Reduce discrete causal compatibility and bounding problems to polynomial programs and certify epsilon-sharpness through dual relaxations and branch-and-bound.
method_exemplars.why_closest: For discrete covariates and outcomes, it supplies an independent sharpness certificate for an analytic support-aware data-fusion formula.

literature_map:
Lanners, Rudin, Volfovsky & Parikh (NeurIPS 2025; arXiv:2505.24296) introduce joint rho-gamma sensitivity bounds, but their compatibility diagnostic averages conditional overlap and fixes fitted nuisances in resampling.
Parikh et al. (2023; arXiv:2307.01449) give DML diagnostics and a no-free-lunch result: fusion needs correct attribution of external-validity versus ignorability failure.
Hussain et al. (AISTATS 2023, PMLR 206) formulate internal/external-validity falsification as conditional moment restrictions, reinforcing that compatibility is conditional rather than only an averaged discrepancy.
Dahabreh et al. (Statistics in Medicine 2026; arXiv:2207.09982; doi:10.1002/sim.70083) parameterize study-exchangeability violations globally but do not combine them with observational NUC violations.
Bonvini & Kennedy (JASA 2022; doi:10.1080/01621459.2020.1864382) and Bonvini et al. (arXiv:2210.04681) give efficient sensitivity-bound inference for unmeasured confounding, not joint data-source exchangeability failure.
Masten, Poirier & Zhang (JBES 2024; arXiv:2012.15716; doi:10.1080/07350015.2023.2183212) obtain uniform bands over a scalar unconfoundedness sensitivity parameter.
Masten & Poirier (Quantitative Economics 2020; arXiv:1705.04765; doi:10.3982/QE1288) provide multidimensional breakdown-frontier inference, but not an exact compatibility zero set with learned data-fusion nuisances.
Masten, Poirier & Ren (2025; arXiv:2501.15400) unify relaxations of unconfoundedness and derive sharp distributional bounds, while study exchangeability remains outside that class.
Chernozhukov, Lee & Rosen (Econometrica 2013; arXiv:0907.3503; doi:10.3982/ECTA8718) supply intersection-bound and adaptive contact-set inference for continuum inequalities.
Andrews & Soares (Econometrica 2010; doi:10.3982/ECTA7502) supply uniform generalized moment selection and local-power tools when the binding set changes.
Fang & Santos (Review of Economic Studies 2019; doi:10.1093/restud/rdy049) explain ordinary-bootstrap failure for directionally differentiable max and positive-part maps.
Luedtke & van der Laan (Annals of Statistics 2016; arXiv:1603.07573; doi:10.1214/15-AOS1384) isolate nonregularity at positive-mass treatment ties, the closest analogue to interval or arm ties here.
Chernozhukov et al. (Econometrics Journal 2018; arXiv:1608.00060; doi:10.1111/ectj.12097) provide the orthogonal-score and cross-fitting substrate needed to propagate flexible nuisance uncertainty.
Levis et al. (JRSSB 2025; arXiv:2301.12106; doi:10.1093/jrsssb/qkaf028) develop exact and smoothed inference for nonsmooth covariate-assisted bounds under margin conditions.
Ben-Michael (2025; arXiv:2506.12215) treats conditional-LP bounds with exact dual debiasing and entropic smoothing, while the support-aware sharp program and finite-alpha error remain unresolved.
prior_proposal_map:
The current pid_datafusion_exact_compatibility directory contains state and orchestrator files but no prior proposal or reviewer JSON.
The highest-yield adjacent source was banked pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec/v2, which studies two simultaneous restrictions on one latent law.
Its §11 scope flag makes identification conditional on joint feasibility, while its README records uniform constrained-class argmax consistency as an unresolved inference gate.
Reviewer angle0_v4 prescribes a directional-differentiability problem for active-set switching; angle0_v3 requests the missing external-sample influence contribution.
The earlier pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec/v1 proposal and reviews were inspected for finite-grid nonrectangular compatibility and cancellation by separate optimization.
Downgraded eid_data_fusion_k_source/v1 and failed eid_fragmented_transport_support/v1 were inspected but concern graphical source-overlap rather than this sensitivity frontier.
Rejected sensitivity angles under pid_policy_regret_ch_sensitivity/v1 and pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity/v1 were also checked and were less directly aligned than the joint-misspecification feasibility and contact gaps.
